behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch recursion behavior (OS watcher semantics + daemon wiring). A fake watcher can emit synthetic subdirectory events and always pass, while real backends often differ on recursive delivery. REAL-INFRA is yes (real FS events), so System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem/event handling, with no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets dedupe decision logic itself (same path + window), which is a local state/time rule and does not require OS/network/subprocess. MOCK-MISS scoped to this behavior: failure can be exposed at function/object boundary without cross-module handoff. Rejected higher levels: text does not require real filesystem events; it says "deduplication logic" and "same file path within a configurable time window," which can be validated directly with controlled timestamps.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedupe component with a known window, submit path A at t0 and again at t0+delta (delta < window), assert second request is rejected; then at t0+window+epsilon assert acceptance.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate events. REAL-INFRA is yes, and MOCK-HIDE is yes: synthetic single-event or idealized fake event streams can miss backend-specific duplicate bursts/coalescing behavior. Therefore System is the minimum to catch real failures. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern that triggers multiple native events (e.g., write+flush+rename sequence), capture processing count, and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level watcher lifecycle behavior (inode/handle invalidation on delete/recreate). In-process fakes typically do not reproduce real watch descriptor loss/rebind semantics, so MOCK-HIDE is yes and REAL-INFRA is yes. Minimum is System. Rejected higher levels: behavior is filesystem recovery only, with no AI/ML output dependence or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed successfully without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamps plus event arrival under concurrent writes. Integration can validate sorting logic, but may miss real failures from timestamp granularity/tie behavior and native event timing; REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are both likely yes for this wording. So minimum is System. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision quality or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the real watched directory with controlled timestamp differences where possible, run daemon, and assert processing/output order matches creation-time ordering policy (including tie-break rule if defined).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between native FS events and write-completion detection. Fakes often model writes atomically and hide partial-write races; REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are yes. Minimum is System. Rejected higher levels: behavior has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real watched directory, open a file and stream-write chunks slowly while keeping it incomplete, verify daemon does not process mid-write, then close/finish write and assert processing happens once after completion.
